titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Pierce Baker Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 9371333.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 13, 2020 at 06:45

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The George Pierce Baker Papers contain scripts from a scenario workshop that he taught at Yale and student papers from other courses.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Early acquistion information unknown. Transferred from Yale School of Drama, 2007.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The George Pierce Baker Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George Pierce Baker Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call numbers: Uncat ZA MS Baker and Uncat MSS 940.

bioghist.head: George Pierce Baker (1866-1935)
bioghist.p: George Pierce Baker was an American drama educator. He graduated from Harvard University in 1887 and from 1888 to 1924 was a faculty member in the English Department. While at Harvard, he played a key role in starting the Harvard Theatre Collection at Harvard University Library; he created the Harvard Dramatic Club; and he founded Workshop 47 to provide a forum for the performance of plays developed for his English 47 class. Baker was unable to convince Harvard to offer a degree in playwrighting and in 1925 he accepted a position at Yale University. At Yale, he helped found the Yale School of Drama and remained there until his retirement in 1933.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The George Pierce Baker Papers contain scripts from a scenario workshop that he taught at Yale and student papers from other courses.

did.unittitle: Bates, Esther. "For Your Freedom and Ours: The Case of Poland"
Fuller, Lois C. "A Right to Live"
Hawkridge, Winifred. "The Florist Shop, Alias the Price of Orchids"
Hinkley, Eleanor Holmes. "A Flitch of Bacon"
List of student paper titles and authors and notes on unidentified scripts
"The Actor-Manager of the Eighteenth Century and His Effect on the Drama"
"Continuation of the first part of the thesis ending with 'The wise woman of" Hodgson'
"The Dramatic Craftsmanship of Mr Bernard Shaw"
"A History of American Revivals of Elizabethan and Jacobean Plays"
"Laporte, Cloyd. The Late Work of Shakespeare"
"The Principal Characters of the Three Last Plays of Shakespeare"
"The Relation of the Elizabethan Satire to the Satirical Comedies of the Period"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged and produced by George Pierce Baker.

did.unittitle: "The Sources, Date and Authorship of 'The famous Victories of Henry V' "
"Technique of the Drama of Thomas Middleton: Bibliography"
"Third part of the trilogy 'Nightfall on the terrace'"
